title: Groups, Profiles, and Licensing
description: Understand how Windchill organizes users into groups, assigns licenses through license groups, and controls UI visibility through license and standard profiles.

topics.title: User and Group Structure

content.text: A Windchill site must contain at least one organization, and within that organization, users and groups form a structured hierarchy. Understanding this hierarchy is essential for managing access, applying policies, and organizing your team effectively.

rows: The top-level container for all Windchill objects and organizations.
Every Windchill deployment has exactly one site context.
A principal that acts as a container for users, groups, and policies.
A site must have at least one organization; the organization principal includes all associated users.
An organizational-level set of users or other groups.
Groups can contain both individual users and nested groups, enabling flexible membership structures.
An individual account representing a person who accesses the system.
Users can belong to multiple groups and must be associated with an organization.

content.text: The organization principal acts like a supergroup — its membership automatically includes all users associated with that organization. This makes it convenient for applying broad rules and policies without manually adding every user.

content.prompt: Arrange the Windchill hierarchy from broadest scope to most specific:

keyTakeaways: A Windchill site must contain at least one organization, which acts as the top-level container for users and groups.
The organization principal functions as a supergroup that automatically includes all associated users.
Groups are organizational-level constructs that can contain both users and other groups.
The hierarchy flows from Site to Organization to Group to User, each level narrowing the scope.

topics.title: PTC Windchill PLM Licenses

content.text: Windchill functionality is organized into license packages, each grouping a set of capabilities together. Every user must have an assigned license to access the system, and understanding which packages provide which capabilities is critical for proper system administration.

cards.back: Core PDM, Visualization (AR/3D/2D), Project Planning, Document Management, Change Management, PTC Non-CAD Integration.
Engineering BOM creation, filtering, and management capabilities.
BOM restructuring and manufacturing view generation.
Base set of capabilities and prerequisite to all licenses except PTC Contributor and SLM packages.

content.text: Every user must have an assigned license to access Windchill. Without a license assignment, users cannot log in or perform any actions in the system.

content.prompt: Match each capability description to the correct Windchill license package:

keyTakeaways: Windchill functionality is organized into license packages that group related capabilities together.
The Windchill Base package includes Core PDM, Visualization, Project Planning, Document Management, Change Management, and PTC Non-CAD Integration.
The PTC Author License is a prerequisite to all other licenses except PTC Contributor and SLM packages.
Every user must have an assigned license to access the Windchill system.

topics.title: License Groups and User Licensing

content.text: License groups are the mechanism through which licenses are assigned to users in Windchill. Understanding the difference between Named User and Active Daily User license types — and how the License Exclusion group works — is essential for managing license capacity and cost effectively.
Named User vs Active Daily User Licenses

rows: Unlimited members can be added to the group
When at capacity, no additional users can be added
Concurrent daily usage is tracked against purchased count
Persistent — assigned user always has access
Users who need guaranteed, consistent access
Large teams with variable daily usage patterns

content.text: The License Exclusion group allows a user to be licensed without counting against the purchased license total. This is ideal for testing and administrative accounts and is only available at the site context level.

Users must be added to the appropriate license group to receive their license. If a user is a member of both a Named User and an Active Daily User license group, they can sign in at any time — the Named User license guarantees access regardless of the daily concurrent limit.

content.prompt: Match each licensing characteristic to the correct license type or group:

pairs.left: Fixed number of users in the group

keyTakeaways: Named User licenses assign a fixed number of specific users, while Active Daily User licenses allow unlimited members with concurrent daily tracking.
Active Daily User licenses reset at 23:59:59 GMT, freeing up capacity for the next day.
The License Exclusion group enables users to be licensed without consuming purchased license count, ideal for testing and administration.
A user who belongs to both Named User and Active Daily User groups can sign in at any time.

topics.title: License Profiles and Profile Interaction

content.text: Each license group in Windchill has a corresponding license profile that controls which UI actions are visible to users. Understanding how license profiles and standard profiles interact determines what users can see and do in the system.

License profiles are PTC-maintained and read-only — they define the default set of UI actions available for each license type. If you need to customize visibility beyond what the license profile provides, you can save it as a standard profile and modify the copy.

License profiles are PTC-maintained and read-only. To customize action visibility, save the license profile as a standard profile and edit the copy. The PTC Author License is a prerequisite to all licenses except PTC Contributor and SLM packages.

rows: Action Yes in any license profile
Action Yes in any standard profile
Action No in standard profile sum

content.prompt: Arrange the steps showing how Windchill evaluates and combines profiles:
items: Combine: No in either sum hides the action

keyTakeaways: Each license group has a corresponding license profile that controls UI action visibility for its members.
License profiles are PTC-maintained and read-only; save as a standard profile to customize.
When combining profile sums, a No in either the license sum or the standard profile sum will hide the action.
Yes prevails within each profile type (license or standard), but No prevails when combining across types.

topics.title: Exercise - Assign Users to License Groups

content.text: In this exercise, you will assign newly created user accounts to a license group so they can access the Windchill system. Without a license group assignment, users cannot log in or perform any actions.
Before starting this exercise, ensure you are signed in as wcadmin with administrative privileges.

content.title: Assign Users to a License Group
content.objective: Assign the newly created user accounts to the PTC PDMLink Module License group to grant them system access.

tasks.title: Assign Users to a License Group

steps.detail: Browse to PTC Publications > Profiles in the Navigator.

steps.action: Open the PTC PDMLink Module License profile
steps.detail: Locate the PTC PDMLink Module License profile in the License Profiles table and click the View information icon.
Scroll to the Profile Members table and click the PTC PDMLink Module License group link.
Click Actions > Edit Group and browse to Step 2 to add members.

steps.action: Add the new users to the group
steps.detail: Click the Add members to group icon, search for amansor and asanthosh, and add them to the group.
steps.hint: You can search for multiple users and add them in a single operation.

steps.detail: Click OK and then Finish to complete the license group assignment.

keyTakeaways: Users must be assigned to a license group before they can access Windchill.
License group membership is managed through the License Profiles area under PTC Publications > Profiles.
Multiple users can be searched for and added to a license group in a single operation.
The PTC PDMLink Module License group grants access to core PDMLink capabilities.
